NYK_side.assumption: NYK model favored despite market showing CLE home side resistance; NYK +2.5 / NYK spread was primary edge.

NYK_team_total.assumption: NYK offense upgraded through multi-lane scoring; projected 111.8 mean.

CLE_team_total.assumption: CLE creation fragility and above-break three dependency made CLE overs untrustworthy; CLE mean 108.4 with under preference at inflated numbers.

game_total.assumption: Full-game under was caution because NYK could break total alone; projected total 220.2.

Q1.notes: NYK flipped early CLE lead with transition, offensive glass, and bench rim pressure.

Q2.notes: CLE turnover spiral created NYK runout scoring and expanded deficit to 19 at half.

Q3.notes: NYK sustained advantage; Cleveland failed to stabilize half-court creation.

Q4.notes: Blowout state did not generate meaningful CLE recovery; NYK continued scoring through secondary lanes.

rebound_environment_analysis.status: NYK offensive glass and rebound-control signal confirmed

player_notes_through_3q.Karl-Anthony Towns: 15 points, 13 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 steals, 2 blocks through 3Q; rebound downgrade should be modified, not reverted, because Game 4 created a favorable rebound environment from CLE misses and NYK control.
player_notes_through_3q.Josh Hart: 6 points, 10 rebounds, 6 assists through 3Q; utility/rebound role strongly validated.
player_notes_through_3q.Mitchell Robinson: 8 points, 9 rebounds in 16:56 through 3Q; bench-big rebound spike and offensive glass impact confirmed.
player_notes_through_3q.Mikal Bridges: 15 points through 3Q; 15+ points prop hit, but volume was inefficient at 4/16 through 3Q, so scoring edge was role/volume based rather than efficiency based.

Karl-Anthony Towns.status: rebounds_over_projection; scoring below full-game mean but game state reduced need

Mikal Bridges.status: 15_plus_hit; full mean uncertain from provided final card

OG Anunoby.status: near_projection; defensive/transition contribution exceeded scoring-only view

Donovan Mitchell.status: individual scoring hit despite team collapse

James Harden.status: assist projection failed; creation fragility and turnover risk strongly confirmed

Evan Mobley.status: near but suppressed by team offensive breakdown

Jarrett Allen.status: projection miss; role/usage collapsed in NYK pressure environment

Sam Merrill.status: bench spacing not enough to offset collapse

patch_evaluation.CAVS_CREATION_FRAGILITY_CONFIRMED: KEEP — Cleveland produced 16 turnovers through 3Q and 93 final points.
patch_evaluation.CLE_ABOVE_BREAK_THREE_DEPENDENCY_ON: KEEP — CLE shot 26.7% from 3 through 3Q and could not create stable rim/paint scoring.
patch_evaluation.NYK_MULTI_LANE_OFFENSE_UPGRADE: KEEP — NYK reached 130 with Brunson not needing to carry the scoring load.
patch_evaluation.KAT_REBOUND_FUNNEL_DOWNGRADE: MODIFY — downgrade was too conservative for Game 4 environment; add conditional override when CLE miss volume + NYK offensive glass edge is active.
patch_evaluation.TEAM_TOTAL_UNDER_CAN_DECOUPLE_FROM_GAME_: KEEP — CLE under hit while full-game total landed over model lean zone because NYK exploded.
patch_evaluation.LEAD_SUPPRESSION_TOTAL_CAUTION: KEEP — game total still reached 223 despite 37-point margin.

rolling_window_update.series_pattern: NYK control now strengthened across side, team total, offensive glass, and Cavs creation fragility.
rolling_window_update.recommendation_for_game_5_prep: Open with NYK side/TT bias, CLE TT under bias at inflated numbers, avoid Harden assists unless line is discounted, and reprice KAT/Hart/Robinson rebounds using CLE miss-volume environment.

final_status: KEEP core patches; MODIFY KAT rebound downgrade with conditional environment override
